author: doll-agent
content:
Implemented hybrid 2D (M x N) parallelism grid in ferray-linalg/src/gemm/outer.rs.

Architecture: gemm_avx2_f64 now picks a (mw, nw) factorisation of the 8-thread pool's worker count and dispatches one tile of C per worker via rayon::par_iter. Each worker still runs gemm_seq_avx2_f64 on its sub-rectangle, with thread-local A/B-pack buffers (#656).

pick_grid(m, n, k, workers) heuristic:
  1. Default to 1D M-slab (workers, 1) for square/tall shapes — gives every worker its own non-overlapping range of A and C with no redundant pack_a.
  2. Switch to 2D when EITHER:
     - m / MR < workers (M too small to give every worker an MR-aligned slab), OR
     - 1D's per-worker B-pack (kc * n * 8B) > 1 MB L2 budget — at that point 2D's N-split keeps the per-worker working set in L2.
  3. Among 2D candidates, picks the factorisation minimising |log(per_m) - log(per_n)|, with mw <= max(m/MR, 1) and ties broken toward larger mw.

Performance picture (median of 30 iters, NumPy MKL vs ferray, 14700K):
| shape                    | NumPy GFLOPs | ferray GFLOPs | grid |
| 256x256x256              | 379          | 71            | (8,1) |
| 512x512x512              | 567          | 183           | (8,1) |
| Short-fat 64x1024x256    | 384          | 99            | (1,8) (M too small for 1D) |
| Short-fat 64x2048x256    | 415          | 140           | (1,8) |
| Short-fat 128x1024x256   | 409          | 139           | (4,2)/(8,1) (L2 budget) |
| Tall-skinny 1024x64x256  | 425          | 100           | (8,1) |
| Tall-skinny 2048x64x256  | 472          | 146           | (8,1) |
| Wide rect 256x1024x256   | 463          | 185           | (8,1) |
| Tall rect 1024x256x256   | 560          | 188           | (8,1) |

Key finding: **on square matrices 1D wins decisively over 2D**, because 2D doubles A-read traffic (workers in the same M-row group read the same A panel from L3/DRAM while workers in 1D read disjoint A slabs). The 2D path activates on its merits when either M is too small for 8-way M-split or when 1D's B-pack would overflow L2 — preventing regression on shapes where 1D would otherwise hit a cliff.

Tested as part of 207 ferray-linalg tests + 47/47 statistical_equivalence.py — accuracy unchanged. matmul 10x10 max ULP 81, 100x100 max ULP 11264 (both within thresholds).

Verdict: 2D infrastructure is in place and correct, but **for the 14700K's cache hierarchy and typical square workloads, 1D M-slab remains the best dispatch**. The 2D path is a safety net for shapes where 1D fails the L2 budget — it keeps performance from cliff-falling on short-fat or huge-N cases. Defensive value > average-case speed-up here.
